message.content:
Search the codebase and read:
1. src/services/index.ts - to see how services are exported
2. One complete service file (e.g., src/services/service-demand.ts) to understand the coding patterns
3. src/lib/services/base.ts - the base service class

Provide the complete code for each file so I can understand the patterns used.

content.text: I'll search the codebase to find and read these service-related files.
Now let me read these three files:

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * MMS Domain Services
     3→ *
     4→ * Central export for all domain services in the MMS system.
     5→ * Each service encapsulates business logic for a specific domain.
     6→ *
     7→ * Services extend BaseService for:
     8→ * - OpenTelemetry distributed tracing
     9→ * - Structured logging with Pino
    10→ * - Database transaction management
    11→ * - Consistent error handling
    12→ *
    13→ * @see specs/004-domain-services.md
    14→ */
    15→
    16→// =============================================================================
    17→// Fleet Service
    18→// =============================================================================
    19→export {
    20→  FleetService,
    21→  getFleetService,
    22→  type FleetListFilters,
    23→  type TrainListFilters,
    24→  type PaginationOptions,
    25→  type PaginatedResult,
    26→  type FleetStatistics,
    27→  type TrainWithFleet,
    28→  type FitnessStatusChangeRequest,
    29→  type MileageUpdateRequest,
    30→} from './fleet';
    31→
    32→// =============================================================================
    33→// Unavailability Service
    34→// =============================================================================
    35→export {
    36→  UnavailabilityService,
    37→  getUnavailabilityService,
    38→  type CreateEventRequest,
    39→  type ResolveEventRequest,
    40→  type EventListFilters,
    41→  type PMConflict,
    42→  type EventWithTrain,
    43→  type OptimizerConstraints,
    44→} from './unavailability';
    45→
    46→// =============================================================================
    47→// Service Demand Service
    48→// =============================================================================
    49→export {
    50→  ServiceDemandService,
    51→  getServiceDemandService,
    52→  type DayType,
    53→  type TimePeriod,
    54→  type DemandConfig,
    55→  type AvailabilityValidation,
    56→  type DemandViolation,
    57→  type ProposedPM,
    58→  type CreateDemandRequest,
    59→  type DemandListFilters,
    60→  type FleetAvailabilitySummary,
    61→} from './service-demand';
    62→
    63→// =============================================================================
    64→// Route Utilization Service
    65→// =============================================================================
    66→export {
    67→  RouteUtilizationService,
    68→  getRouteUtilizationService,
    69→  type UtilizationDayType,
    70→  type ConfidenceLevel,
    71→  type RateSource,
    72→  type DailyRateResult,
    73→  type MileageProjection,
    74→  type RouteProfileWithMeta,
    75→  type CreateProfileRequest,
    76→  type UpdateProfileRequest,
    77→  type ProfileListFilters,
    78→  type FleetAverageResult,
    79→} from './route-utilization';
    80→
    81→// =============================================================================
    82→// Ingestion Service
    83→// =============================================================================
    84→export {
    85→  IngestionService,
    86→  getIngestionService,
    87→  type AnomalyType,
    88→  type DbAnomalyType,
    89→  type AnomalyRecord,
    90→  type MeterEventRecord,
    91→  type FileWatcherConfig,
    92→  type FileValidationResult,
    93→  type IngestionResult,
    94→  type MileageReadingFilters,
    95→  type MileageReadingWithTrain,
    96→  type AnomalyQueryFilters,
    97→  type AnomalyWithDetails,
    98→} from './ingestion';
    99→
   100→// =============================================================================
   101→// PM Scheduler Service
   102→// =============================================================================
   103→export {
   104→  PMSchedulerService,
   105→  getPMSchedulerService,
   106→  type PmDueResult,
   107→  type ComplianceWindow,
   108→  type MaterialAvailability,
   109→  type MaterialConstraintResult,
   110→  type TrackAllocation,
   111→  type SchedulingResultItem,
   112→  type SchedulingExplanation,
   113→  type SchedulingResult,
   114→  type SchedulePmRequest,
   115→  type MovePmRequest,
   116→  type TacticalScheduleRequest,
   117→} from './pm-scheduler';
   118→
   119→// =============================================================================
   120→// Baseline Service
   121→// =============================================================================
   122→export {
   123→  BaselineService,
   124→  getBaselineService,
   125→  type BaselineStatus,
   126→  type BaselineListFilters,
   127→  type BaselinePaginationOptions,
   128→  type PaginatedBaselineResult,
   129→  type PublishBaselineRequest,
   130→  type PlanValidationResult,
   131→  type PlanConflict,
   132→  type PlanWarning,
   133→  type BaselineSummary,
   134→  type BaselinePmScheduleWithTrain,
   135→  type BaselineMileageProjectionWithTrain,
   136→} from './baseline';
   137→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
